title: achillea aleppica subsp. altinozlui
intro: Achillea aleppica subsp. altinozlui is a subspecies of the Achillea aleppica plant, which belongs to the family Asteraceae and is commonly known as a type of yarrow. This perennial herb is native to Turkey and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, menstrual cramps, and skin conditions, due to its ability to ease pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Achillea aleppica subsp. altinozlui can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its health benefits.
